cli_commands.name: download-csv
cli_commands.help: Download an aggregated instance usage report as a zipped CSV to the provided directory.

options.name: start_date
options.help: The start date (inclusive) of the aggregated instance usage report. Format: YYYY-MM-DD

options.name: end_date
options.help: The end date (inclusive) of the aggregated instance usage report. Format: YYYY-MM-DD

options.name: cloud
options.help: The name of the cloud to filter the report by.

options.name: project
options.help: The name of the project to filter the report by.

options.name: directory
options.help: The directory to save the CSV file to. Default is the current directory.

options.name: hide_progress_bar
options.help: Whether to hide the progress bar. Default is False.

options.name: help
options.help: Show this message and exit.

examples.cli:
$ anyscale aggregated-instance-usage download-csv --start-date 2024-10-01 --end-date 2024-10-03
(anyscale +0.5s) Downloading aggregated instance usage CSV...
Download complete! File saved to 'aggregated_instance_usage_2024-10-01_2024-10-03.zip'
0:00:01 100% ━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━ 79.4 kB / 79.4 kB Downloading aggregated instance usage CSV
(anyscale +2.2s) Downloaded CSV to aggregated_instance_usage_2024-10-01_2024-10-03.zip

sdk_commands.name: download_csv
sdk_commands.docstring: Download an aggregated instance usage report as a zipped CSV to the provided directory.

parameters.name: filters
parameters.docstring: The filter of the instance usage to download.

examples.python:
import anyscale
from anyscale.aggregated_instance_usage.models import DownloadCSVFilters

anyscale.aggregated_instance_usage.download_csv(
    DownloadCSVFilters(
        start_date="2024-10-01",
        end_date="2024-10-02",
        cloud="cloud_name",
        project="project_name",
        directory="/directory",
        hide_progress_bar=False,
    ),
)

models.name: DownloadCSVFilters
models.docstring: Filters to use when downloading usage CSVs.

fields.name: start_date
fields.docstring: Start date (UTC inclusive) for the usage CSV.

fields.name: end_date
fields.docstring: End date (UTC inclusive) for the usage CSV.

fields.name: cloud
fields.docstring: Optional cloud name to filter by.

fields.name: project
fields.docstring: Optional project name to filter by.

fields.name: directory
fields.docstring: Optional directory to save the CSV to.

fields.name: hide_progress_bar
fields.docstring: Optional hide progress bar.

examples.python:
import anyscale
from anyscale.aggregated_instance_usage.models import DownloadCSVFilters

download_csv_filters = DownloadCSVFilters(
    # Start date (UTC inclusive) for the usage CSV.
    start_date="2024-10-01",
    # End date (UTC inclusive) for the usage CSV.
    end_date="2024-10-31",
    # Optional cloud name to filter by.
    cloud="cloud_name",
    # Optional project name to filter by.
    project="project_name",
    # Optional directory to save the CSV to.
    directory="/directory",
    # Optional hide progress bar.
    hide_progress_bar=False,
)

cli_commands.name: batch-create
cli_commands.help: Batch create, as an admin, users without email verification.

options.name: users_file
options.help: Path to a YAML file that contains the information for user accounts to be created.

options.name: help
options.help: Show this message and exit.

examples.cli:
$ anyscale user batch-create --users-file users.yaml
(anyscale +0.5s) Creating users...
(anyscale +0.8s) 2 users created.

$ cat users_file.yaml
create_users:
  - name: name1
    email: test1@anyscale.com
    password: ''
    is_sso_user: false
    lastname: lastname1
    title: title1
  - name: name2
    email: test2@anyscale.com
    password: ''
    is_sso_user: false
    lastname: lastname2
    title: title2

examples.python:
from typing import Iterator

import anyscale
from anyscale.project.models import Project, ProjectSortField, ProjectSortOrder

projects: Iterator[Project] = anyscale.project.list(
    name_contains="my-project",
    creator_id="my-user-id",
    parent_cloud_id="my-cloud-id",
    include_defaults=True,
    max_items=20,
    page_size=10,
    sort_field=ProjectSortField.NAME,
    sort_order=ProjectSortOrder.ASC,
)
for project in projects:
    print(project.name)

cli_commands.name: submit
cli_commands.help:
Submit a job.

    The job config can be specified in one of the following ways:

    * Job config file can be specified as a single positional argument. E.g. `anyscale job submit config.yaml`.

    * Job config can also be specified with command-line arguments. In this case, the entrypoint should be specified
as the positional arguments starting with `--`. Other arguments can be specified with command-line flags. E.g.

      * `anyscale job submit -- python main.py`: submit a job with the entrypoint `python main.py`.

      * `anyscale job submit --name my-job -- python main.py`: submit a job with the name `my-job` and the
entrypoint `python main.py`.

    * [Experimental] If you want to specify a config file and override some arguments with the commmand-line flags,
use the `--config-file` flag. E.g.

      * `anyscale job submit --config-file config.yaml`: submit a job with the config in `config.yaml`.

      * `anyscale job submit --config-file config.yaml -- python main.py`: submit a job with the config in `config.yaml`
and override the entrypoint with `python main.py`.

    Either containerfile or image-uri should be used, specifying both will result in an error.

    By default, this command submits the job asynchronously and exits. To wait for the job to complete, use the `--wait` flag.

options.name: name
options.help: Name of the job.

options.name: wait
options.help: Block this CLI command and print logs until the job finishes.

options.name: config_file
options.help: Path to a YAML config file to use for this job. Command-line flags will overwrite values read from the file.

options.name: compute_config
options.help: Named compute configuration to use for the job. This defaults to the compute configuration of the workspace.

options.name: image_uri
options.help: Container image to use for this job. When running in a workspace, this defaults to the image of the workspace.

options.name: registry_login_secret
options.help: Name or identifier of the secret containing credentials to authenticate to the docker registry hosting the image. This can only be used when 'image_uri' is specified and the image is not hosted on Anyscale.

options.name: containerfile
options.help: Path to a containerfile to build the image to use for the job.

options.name: env
options.help: Environment variables to set for the job. The format is 'key=value'. This argument can be specified multiple times. When the same key is also specified in the config file, the value from the command-line flag will overwrite the value from the config file.

options.name: working_dir
options.help: Path to a local directory or a remote URI to a .zip file (S3, GS, HTTP) that will be the working directory for the job. The files in the directory will be automatically uploaded to cloud storage. When running in a workspace, this defaults to the current working directory.

options.name: exclude
options.help: File pattern to exclude when uploading local directories. This argument can be specified multiple times and the patterns will be appended to the 'excludes' list in the config file (if any).

options.name: requirements
options.help: Path to a requirements.txt file containing dependencies for the job. Anyscale installs these dependencies on top of the image. If you run a job from a workspace, the default is to use the workspace dependencies, but specifying this option overrides them.

options.name: py_module
options.help: Python modules to be available for import in the Ray workers. Each entry must be a path to a local directory.

options.name: tags
options.help: Tag in key=value (or key:value) format. Repeat to add multiple.

options.name: cloud
options.help: The Anyscale Cloud to run this workload on. If not provided, the organization default will be used (or, if running in a workspace, the cloud of the workspace).

options.name: project
options.help: Named project to use for the job. If not provided, the default project for the cloud will be used (or, if running in a workspace, the project of the workspace).

options.name: ray_version
options.help: The Ray version (X.Y.Z) to the image specified by --image-uri. This is only used when --image-uri is provided. If you don't specify a Ray version, Anyscale defaults to the latest Ray version available at the time of the Anyscale CLI/SDK release.

options.name: max_retries
options.help: Maximum number of retries to attempt before failing the entire job.

options.name: timeout_s
options.help: The timeout in seconds for each job run. Set to None for no limit to be set.

options.name: help
options.help: Show this message and exit.

examples.cli:
# Submit a job without a yaml file, this will inherit the environment from the workspace.
$ anyscale job submit --name my-job --wait -- python main.py
Output
(anyscale +1.0s) Submitting job with config JobConfig(name='my-job', image_uri=None, compute_config=None, env_vars=None, py_modules=None, cloud=None, project=None, ray_version=None, job_queue_config=None).
(anyscale +1.7s) Uploading local dir '.' to cloud storage.
(anyscale +2.6s) Including workspace-managed pip dependencies.
(anyscale +3.2s) Job 'my-job' submitted, ID: 'prodjob_6ntzknwk1i9b1uw1zk1gp9dbhe'.
(anyscale +3.2s) View the job in the UI: https://console.anyscale.com/jobs/prodjob_6ntzknwk1i9b1uw1zk1gp9dbhe
(anyscale +3.2s) Waiting for the job to run. Interrupting this command will not cancel the job.
(anyscale +3.5s) Waiting for job 'prodjob_6ntzknwk1i9b1uw1zk1gp9dbhe' to reach target state SUCCEEDED, currently in state: STARTING
(anyscale +1m19.7s) Job 'prodjob_6ntzknwk1i9b1uw1zk1gp9dbhe' transitioned from STARTING to SUCCEEDED
(anyscale +1m19.7s) Job 'prodjob_6ntzknwk1i9b1uw1zk1gp9dbhe' reached target state, exiting

# Submit a job specified by a yaml file, this will run a job with the specified settings.
# See https://docs.anyscale.com/reference/job-api/#jobconfig for a list of all available fields.
$ cat job.yaml
name: my-job
entrypoint: python main.py
image_uri: anyscale/ray:2.44.1-slim-py312-cu128 # (Optional) Exclusive with `containerfile`.
# compute_config: # (Optional) An inline dictionary can also be provided.
#   head_node:
#     instance_type: m5.8xlarge
working_dir: . # (Optional) Use current working directory "." as the working_dir. Can be any local path or remote .zip file in cloud storage.
requirements: # (Optional) List of requirements files to install. Can also be a path to a requirements.txt.
  - emoji
max_retries: 0 # (Optional) Maximum number of times the job will be retried before being marked failed. Defaults to `1`.
$ anyscale job submit -f job.yaml

cli_commands.name: status
cli_commands.help:
Query the status of a job.

    To specify the job by name, use the --name flag. To specify the job by id, use the --id flag. Either name or
id should be used, specifying both will result in an error.

    If job is specified by name and there are multiple jobs with the specified name, the most recently created job
status will be returned.

options.name: id
options.help: Unique ID of the job.

options.name: name
options.help: Name of the job.

options.name: cloud
options.help: The Anyscale Cloud to run this workload on. If not provided, the organization default will be used (or, if running in a workspace, the cloud of the workspace).

options.name: project
options.help: Named project to use for the job. If not provided, the default project for the cloud will be used (or, if running in a workspace, the project of the workspace).

options.name: json
options.help: Output the status in a structured JSON format.

options.name: verbose
options.help: Include verbose details in the status.

options.name: help
options.help: Show this message and exit.

examples.cli:
$ anyscale job status -n my-job
id: prodjob_6ntzknwk1i9b1uw1zk1gp9dbhe
name: my-job
state: STARTING
runs:
- name: raysubmit_ynxBVGT1SmzndiXL
  state: SUCCEEDED

cli_commands.name: terminate
cli_commands.help:
Terminate a job.

    To specify the job by name, use the --name flag. To specify the job by id, use the --id flag. Either name or
id should be used, specifying both will result in an error.

    If job is specified by name and there are multiple jobs with the specified name, the most recently created job
status will be terminated.

options.name: id
options.help: Unique ID of the job.

options.name: name
options.help: Name of the job.

options.name: cloud
options.help: The Anyscale Cloud to run this workload on. If not provided, the organization default will be used (or, if running in a workspace, the cloud of the workspace).

options.name: project
options.help: Named project to use for the job. If not provided, the default project for the cloud will be used (or, if running in a workspace, the project of the workspace).

options.name: help
options.help: Show this message and exit.

examples.cli:
$ anyscale job terminate -n my-job
(anyscale +5.4s) Marked job 'my-job' for termination
(anyscale +5.4s) Query the status of the job with `anyscale job status --name my-job`.

cli_commands.name: archive
cli_commands.help:
Archive a job.

    To specify the job by name, use the --name flag. To specify the job by id, use the --id flag. Either name or
id should be used, specifying both will result in an error.

    If job is specified by name and there are multiple jobs with the specified name, the most recently created job
status will be archived.

options.name: id
options.help: Unique ID of the job.

options.name: name
options.help: Name of the job.

options.name: cloud
options.help: The Anyscale Cloud to run this workload on. If not provided, the organization default will be used (or, if running in a workspace, the cloud of the workspace).

options.name: project
options.help: Named project to use for the job. If not provided, the default project for the cloud will be used (or, if running in a workspace, the project of the workspace).

options.name: help
options.help: Show this message and exit.

examples.cli:

(anyscale +8.5s) Job prodjob_vzq2pvkzyz3c1jw55kl76h4dk1 is successfully archived.

options.name: id

options.name: name

options.name: cloud
options.help: The Anyscale Cloud to run this workload on. If not provided, the organization default will be used (or, if running in a workspace, the cloud of the workspace).

options.name: tail

cli_commands.name: wait
cli_commands.help:
Wait for a job to enter a specific state (default: SUCCEEDED).

    To specify the job by name, use the --name flag. To specify the job by id, use the --id flag.

    If the job reaches the target state, the command will exit successfully.

    If the job reaches a terminal state other than the target state, the command will exit with an error.

    If the command reaches the timeout, the command will exit with an error but job execution will continue.

options.name: id
options.help: Unique ID of the job.

options.name: name
options.help: Name of the job.

options.name: cloud
options.help: The Anyscale Cloud to run this workload on. If not provided, the organization default will be used (or, if running in a workspace, the cloud of the workspace).

options.name: project
options.help: Named project to use for the job. If not provided, the default project for the cloud will be used (or, if running in a workspace, the project of the workspace).

options.name: state
options.help: The state to wait for this job to enter

options.name: timeout_s
options.help: The timeout in seconds after which this command will exit.

options.name: help
options.help: Show this message and exit.

examples.cli:
$ anyscale job wait -n my-job
(anyscale +5.7s) Waiting for job 'my-job' to reach target state SUCCEEDED, currently in state: STARTING
(anyscale +1m34.2s) Job 'my-job' transitioned from STARTING to SUCCEEDED
(anyscale +1m34.2s) Job 'my-job' reached target state, exiting

options.name: tags
options.help: This option can be repeated to filter by multiple tags. Tags with the same key are ORed, whereas tags with different keys are ANDed. Example: --tag team:mlops --tag team:infra --tag env:prod. Filters with team: (mlops OR infra) AND env:prod.

options.name: states
options.help: Filter jobs by state. Accepts one or more states. Allowed states: PENDING, AWAITING_CLUSTER_START, UPDATING, RUNNING, SUCCESS, ERRORED, TERMINATED, CLEANING_UP, BROKEN, OUT_OF_RETRIES, RESTARTING

models.name: JobQueueSpec
models.docstring:
Options defining a job queue.

    When the first job with a given job queue spec is submitted, the job queue will be created.
    Subsequent jobs with the same job queue spec will reuse the queue instead of creating another one.

    Jobs can also target an existing queue using the name parameter.

fields.name: idle_timeout_s
fields.docstring: Timeout that the job queue cluster will be kept running while no jobs are running.

fields.name: name
fields.docstring: Name of the job queue that can be used to target it when submitting future jobs. The name of a job queue must be unique within a project.

fields.name: execution_mode
fields.docstring: Execution mode of the jobs submitted into the queue (one of: FIFO,LIFO,PRIORITY

fields.name: compute_config
fields.docstring: The name of an existing compute config that will be used to create the job queue cluster. If not specified, the compute config of the associated job will be used.

fields.name: max_concurrency
fields.docstring: Max number of jobs that can run concurrently.Defaults to 1, meaning only one job can run at a given time.

fields.name: auto_termination_threshold_job_count
fields.docstring: Maximum number of jobs the cluster can run before it becomes eligible for termination

examples.yaml:
job_queue_spec:
    # Unique name that can be used to target this queue by other jobs.
    name: my-job-queue
    execution_mode: FIFO
    # Name of a compute config that will be used to create a cluster to execute jobs in this queue.
    # Must match the compute config of the job if specified.
    compute_config: my-compute-config:1
    max_concurrency: 5
    idle_timeout_s: 3600
examples.python:
import anyscale
from anyscale.job.models import JobQueueSpec, JobQueueExecutionMode

job_queue_spec = JobQueueSpec(
    # Unique name that can be used to target this queue by other jobs.
    name="my-job-queue",
    execution_mode=JobQueueExecutionMode.FIFO,
    # Name of a compute config that will be used to create a cluster to execute jobs in this queue.
    # Must match the compute config of the job if specified.
    compute_config="my-compute-config:1",
    max_concurrency=5,
    idle_timeout_s=3600,
)

models.name: JobQueueConfig
models.docstring: Configuration options for a job related to using a job queue for scheduling and execution.

fields.name: priority
fields.docstring: Job's relative priority (only relevant for Job Queues of type PRIORITY). Valid values range from 0 (highest) to +inf (lowest). Default value is None

fields.name: target_job_queue_name
fields.docstring: The name of an existing job queue to schedule this job in. If this is provided, job_queue_spec cannot be.

fields.name: job_queue_spec
fields.docstring: Configuration options defining a job queue to be created for the job if needed. If this is provided, target_job_queue_name cannot be.

examples.yaml:
# An example configuration that creates a job queue if one does not exist with the provided options.
job_queue_config:
    # Priority of the job (only relevant if the execution_mode is "PRIORITY").
    priority: 100,
    # Specification of the target Job Queue (will be created if does not exist)
    job_queue_spec:
        name: my-job-queue
        compute_config: my-compute-config:1
        idle_timeout_s: 3600

# An example config that targets an existing job queue by name.
job_queue_config:
    # Priority of the job (only relevant if the execution_mode is "PRIORITY").
    priority: 100
    # Name for the job queue this job should be added to (specified in `JobQueueSpec.name`
    # on queue's creation)
    target_job_queue_name: my-new-queue
examples.python:
import anyscale
from anyscale.job.models import JobQueueConfig

# An example configuration that creates a job queue if one does not exist with the provided options.
job_queue_config = JobQueueConfig(
    # Priority of the job (only relevant if the execution_mode is "PRIORITY").
    priority=100,
    # Specification of the target Job Queue (will be created if does not exist)
    job_queue_spec=JobQueueSpec(
      name="my-job-queue",
      compute_config="my-compute-config:1",
      idle_timeout_s=3600,
    ),
)

# An example config that targets an existing job queue by name.
job_queue_config = JobQueueConfig(
    # Job's priority (only relevant for priority queues)
    priority=100,
    # Name for the job queue this job should be added to (specified in `JobQueueSpec.name`
    # on queue's creation)
    target_job_queue_name="my-new-queue"
)

models.name: JobQueueExecutionMode
models.docstring: Execution mode for job queues.

members.name: FIFO
members.docstring: Executes jobs in chronological order ('first in, first out')

members.name: LIFO
members.docstring: Executes jobs in reversed chronological order ('last in, first out')

members.name: PRIORITY
members.docstring: Executes jobs in the order induced by ordering their priorities in ascending order, with 0 being the highest priority

models.name: JobConfig
models.docstring: Configuration options for a job.

fields.name: name
fields.docstring: Name of the job. Multiple jobs can be submitted with the same name.

fields.name: image_uri
fields.docstring: URI of an existing image. Exclusive with `containerfile`.

fields.name: containerfile
fields.docstring: The file path to a containerfile that will be built into an image before running the workload. Exclusive with `image_uri`.

fields.name: compute_config
fields.docstring: The name of an existing registered compute config or an inlined ComputeConfig or MultiResourceComputeConfig object.

fields.name: working_dir
fields.docstring: Directory that will be used as the working directory for the application. If a local directory is provided, it will be uploaded to cloud storage automatically. When running inside a workspace, this defaults to the current working directory ('.').

fields.name: excludes
fields.docstring: A list of file path globs that will be excluded when uploading local files for `working_dir`.

fields.name: requirements
fields.docstring: A list of pip requirements or a path to a `requirements.txt` file for the workload. Anyscale installs these dependencies on top of the image. If you run a workload from a workspace, the default is to use the workspace dependencies, but specifying this option overrides them.

fields.name: env_vars
fields.docstring: A dictionary of environment variables that will be set for the workload.

fields.name: py_modules
fields.docstring: A list of local directories or remote URIs that will be uploaded and added to the Python path.

fields.name: py_executable
fields.docstring: Specifies the executable used for running the Ray workers. It can include arguments as well.

fields.name: cloud
fields.docstring: The Anyscale Cloud to run this workload on. If not provided, the organization default will be used (or, if running in a workspace, the cloud of the workspace).

fields.name: project
fields.docstring: The project for the workload. If not provided, the default project for the cloud will be used (or, if running in a workspace, the project of the workspace).

fields.name: registry_login_secret
fields.docstring: A name or identifier of the secret containing credentials to authenticate to the docker registry hosting the image. This can only be used when 'image_uri' is specified and the image is not hosted on Anyscale.

fields.name: ray_version
fields.docstring: The Ray version (X.Y.Z) specified for this image specified by either an image URI or a containerfile. If you don't specify a Ray version, Anyscale defaults to the latest Ray version available at the time of the Anyscale CLI/SDK release.

fields.name: entrypoint
fields.docstring: Command that will be run to execute the job, e.g., `python main.py`.

fields.name: max_retries
fields.docstring: Maximum number of times the job will be retried before being marked failed. Defaults to `1`.

fields.name: job_queue_config
fields.docstring: Job's configuration related to scheduling & execution using job queues

fields.name: timeout_s
fields.docstring: The timeout in seconds for each job run. Set to None for no limit to be set.

fields.name: tags
fields.docstring: Tags to associate with the job.

examples.yaml:
name: my-job
entrypoint: python main.py
image_uri: anyscale/image/my-image:1 # (Optional) Exclusive with `containerfile`.
containerfile: /path/to/Dockerfile # (Optional) Exclusive with `image_uri`.
compute_config: my-compute-config:1 # (Optional) An inline dictionary can also be provided.
working_dir: /path/to/working_dir # (Optional) Defaults to `.`.
excludes: # (Optional) List of files to exclude from being packaged up for the job.
    - .git
    - .env
    - .DS_Store
    - __pycache__
requirements: # (Optional) List of requirements files to install. Can also be a path to a requirements.txt.
    - emoji==1.2.0
    - numpy==1.19.5
env_vars: # (Optional) Dictionary of environment variables to set in the job.
    MY_ENV_VAR: my_value
    ANOTHER_ENV_VAR: another_value
py_modules: # (Optional) A list of local directories or remote URIs that will be added to the Python path.
    - /path/to/my_module
    - s3://my_bucket/my_module
cloud: anyscale-prod # (Optional) The name of the Anyscale Cloud.
project: my-project # (Optional) The name of the Anyscale Project.
max_retries: 3 # (Optional) Maximum number of times the job will be retried before being marked failed. Defaults to `1`.
tags:
    team: mlops
    purpose: training
examples.python:
from anyscale.job.models import JobConfig

config = JobConfig(
    name="my-job",
    entrypoint="python main.py",
    max_retries=1,
    # An inline `ComputeConfig` or `MultiResourceComputeConfig` can also be provided.
    compute_config="my-compute-config:1",
    # A containerfile path can also be provided.
    image_uri="anyscale/image/my-image:1",
)

models.name: JobState
models.docstring: Current state of a job.

members.name: STARTING
members.docstring: The job is being started and is not yet running.

members.name: RUNNING
members.docstring: The job is running. A job will have state RUNNING if a job run fails and there are remaining retries.

members.name: FAILED
members.docstring: The job did not finish running or the entrypoint returned an exit code other than 0 after retrying up to max_retries times.

members.name: SUCCEEDED
members.docstring: The job finished running and its entrypoint returned exit code 0.

members.name: UNKNOWN
members.docstring: The CLI/SDK received an unexpected state from the API server. In most cases, this means you need to update the CLI.

models.name: JobStatus
models.docstring: Current status of a job.

fields.name: id
fields.docstring: Unique ID of the job (generated when the job is first submitted).

fields.name: name
fields.docstring: Name of the job. Multiple jobs can be submitted with the same name.

fields.name: state
fields.docstring: Current state of the job.

fields.name: config
fields.docstring: Configuration of the job.

fields.name: runs
fields.docstring: List of job run states.

fields.name: creator_id
fields.docstring: ID of the user who created the job.

Create or Update a Schedule

options.help: Path to a YAML config file to use for this schedule. Command-line flags will overwrite values read from the file.

options.name: name
options.help: Name of the schedule.

$ anyscale schedule apply -n my-schedule -f my-schedule.yaml
(anyscale +0.5s) Applying schedule with config ScheduleConfig(job_config=JobConfig(name='my-schedule', image_uri=None, compute_config=None, env_vars=None, py_modules=None, cloud=None, project=None, ray_version=None, job_queue_config=None), cron_expression='0 0 * * * *', timezone='UTC').
(anyscale +2.3s) Uploading local dir '.' to cloud storage.
(anyscale +3.7s) Including workspace-managed pip dependencies.
(anyscale +4.9s) Schedule 'my-schedule' submitted, ID: 'cronjob_vrjrbwcnfjjid7fsld3sfkn8jz'.

$ cat my-schedule.yaml
timezone: local
cron_expression: 0 0 * * * *
job_config:
    name: my-job
    entrypoint: python main.py
    max_retries: 5

cli_commands.name: pause
cli_commands.help:
Pause a Schedule.

    You can pause a schedule by config file, name, or id.

    To specify the schedule by name, use the --name flag. You can specify the cloud with --cloud and the project with --project.

    To specify the schedule by id, use the --id flag.

    To specify the schedule by config file, use --config-file. Ensure that name and optionally cloud and project are specified in the
    config file's job config.

options.name: config_file
options.help: Path to a YAML config file to use for this schedule.

options.name: name
options.help: Name of the schedule.

options.name: id
options.help: Id of the schedule.

options.name: cloud
options.help: The named Anyscale Cloud for the schedule. If not provided, the organization default will be used (or, if running in a workspace, the cloud of the workspace).

options.name: project
options.help: Named project to use for the schedule. If not provided, the default project for the cloud will be used (or, if running in a workspace, the project of the workspace).

options.name: help
options.help: Show this message and exit.

examples.cli:
$ anyscale schedule pause -n my-schedule
(anyscale +3.6s) Set schedule 'my-schedule' to state DISABLED

cli_commands.name: resume
cli_commands.help:
Resume a Schedule

    You can resume a schedule by config file, name, or id.

    To specify the schedule by name, use the --name flag. You can specify the cloud with --cloud and the project with --project.

    To specify the schedule by id, use the --id flag.

    To specify the schedule by config file, use --config-file. Ensure that name and optionally cloud and project are specified in the
    config file's job config.

options.name: config_file

options.name: project
options.help: Named project to use for the schedule. If not provided, the default project for the cloud will be used (or, if running in a workspace, the project of the workspace).

options.name: help
options.help: Show this message and exit.

examples.cli:
$ anyscale schedule resume -n my-schedule
(anyscale +4.1s) Set schedule 'my-schedule' to state ENABLED

    To specify the schedule by id, use the --id flag.

    To specify the schedule by config file, use --config-file. Ensure that name and optionally cloud and project are specified in the

cli_commands.name: run
cli_commands.help:
Manually run a Schedule

    This function takes an existing schedule and runs it now.
    You can specify the schedule by name or id.
    You can also pass in a YAML file as a convinience. This is equivalent to passing in the name specified in the YAML file.
    IMPORTANT: if you pass in a YAML definition that differs from the Schedule defition, the Schedule will NOT be updated.
    Please use the `anyscale schedule apply` command to update the configuration of your schedule
    or use the `anyscale job submit` command to submit a one off job that is not a part of a schedule.

options.name: config_file
options.help: Path to a YAML config file to use for this schedule.

options.name: name
options.help: Name of the schedule.

options.name: id
options.help: Id of the schedule.

options.name: cloud
options.help: The named Anyscale Cloud for the schedule. If not provided, the organization default will be used (or, if running in a workspace, the cloud of the workspace).

options.name: project
options.help: Named project to use for the schedule. If not provided, the default project for the cloud will be used (or, if running in a workspace, the project of the workspace).

options.name: help
options.help: Show this message and exit.

examples.cli:
$ anyscale schedule run -n my-schedule
(anyscale +2.5s) Triggered job for schedule 'my-schedule'.

sdk_commands.name: apply
sdk_commands.docstring:
Apply or update a schedule.

    Returns the id of the schedule.

parameters.name: config
parameters.docstring: The config options defining the schedule.

examples.python:
import anyscale
from anyscale.job.models import JobConfig
from anyscale.schedule.models import ScheduleConfig

anyscale.schedule.apply(
    ScheduleConfig(
        cron_expression="0 0 * * * *",
        job_config=JobConfig(
            name="my-job",
            entrypoint="python main.py",
            working_dir=".",
        )
    )
)

fields.docstring: A cron expression to define the frequency at which to run this cron job, for example '0 0 * * *' is a cron expression that means 'run at midnight'. Visit crontab.guru to construct a precise cron_expression.

fields.docstring: The timezone in which to interpret the cron_expression. Default is Universal time (UTC). You can specify 'local' to use the local timezone

from anyscale.schedule.models import ScheduleConfig

    cron_expression="0 0 * * *"
)

cli_commands.name: list
cli_commands.help: List services.

options.name: service_id
options.help: ID of the service to display.

options.name: name
options.help: Name of the service to display.

options.name: cloud
options.help: The Anyscale Cloud of this workload; defaults to your org/workspace cloud.

options.name: project
options.help: Named project to use; defaults to your org/workspace project.

options.name: tags
options.help: This option can be repeated to filter by multiple tags. Tags with the same key are ORed, whereas tags with different keys are ANDed. Example: --tag team:mlops --tag team:infra --tag env:prod. Filters with team: (mlops OR infra) AND env:prod.

options.name: created_by_me
options.help: List services created by me only.

options.name: state_filter
options.help: Filter by service state (repeatable). Allowed: STARTING, RUNNING, UPDATING, ROLLING_OUT, ROLLING_BACK, UNHEALTHY, TERMINATING, TERMINATED, UNKNOWN, SYSTEM_FAILURE

options.name: include_archived
options.help: Include archived services.

options.name: max_items
options.help: Max total items (only with --no-interactive).

options.name: page_size
options.help: Items per page (max 50).

options.name: interactive
options.help: Use interactive paging.

options.name: sort
options.help: Sort by FIELD (prefix with '-' for desc). Allowed: STATUS, NAME, CREATED_AT

options.name: verbose
options.help: Include full config in JSON output.

options.name: json_output
options.help: Emit structured JSON to stdout.

options.name: help
options.help: Show this message and exit.

examples.cli: $ anyscale service list --state running --sort -created_at

cli_commands.name: deploy
cli_commands.help: Deploy or update a service.

options.name: config_file
options.help: Path to a YAML config file to deploy. When deploying from a file, import path and arguments cannot be provided. Command-line flags will overwrite values read from the file.

options.name: name
options.help: Unique name for the service. When running in a workspace, this defaults to the workspace name.

options.name: image_uri
options.help: Container image to use for the service. This cannot be changed when using --in-place and is exclusive with --containerfile. When running in a workspace, this defaults to the image of the workspace.

options.name: registry_login_secret
options.help: Name or identifier of the secret containing credentials to authenticate to the docker registry hosting the image. This can only be used when 'image_uri' is specified and the image is not hosted on Anyscale.

options.name: containerfile
options.help: Path to a containerfile to build the image to use for the service. This cannot be changed when using --in-place and is exclusive with --image-uri.

options.name: ray_version
options.help: The Ray version (X.Y.Z) to the image specified by --image-uri. This is only used when --image-uri is provided. If you don't specify a Ray version, Anyscale defaults to the latest Ray version available at the time of the Anyscale CLI/SDK release.

options.name: compute_config
options.help: Named compute configuration to use for the service. This cannot be changed when using --in-place. When running in a workspace, this defaults to the compute configuration of the workspace.

options.name: working_dir
options.help: Path to a local directory or a remote URI to a .zip file (S3, GS, HTTP) that will be the working directory for the service. The files in the directory will be automatically uploaded to cloud storage. When running in a workspace, this defaults to the current working directory.

options.name: exclude
options.help: File pattern to exclude when uploading local directories. This argument can be specified multiple times and the patterns will be appended to the 'excludes' list in the config file (if any).

options.name: requirements
options.help: Path to a requirements.txt file containing dependencies for the service. Anyscale installs these dependencies on top of the image. If you deploy a service from a workspace, the default is to use the workspace dependencies, but specifying this option overrides them.

options.name: in_place
options.help: Perform an in-place upgrade without starting a new cluster. This can be used for faster iteration during development but is *not* currently recommended for production deploys. This *cannot* be used to change cluster-level options such as image and compute config (they will be ignored).

options.name: canary_percent
options.help: The percentage of traffic to send to the canary version of the service (0-100). This can be used to manually shift traffic toward (or away from) the canary version. If not provided, traffic will be shifted incrementally toward the canary version until it reaches 100. Not supported when using --in-place. This is ignored when restarting a service or creating a new service.

options.name: max_surge_percent
options.help: Amount of excess capacity allowed to be used while updating the service (0-100). Defaults to 100. Not supported when using --in-place.

options.name: env
options.help: Environment variables to set for the service. The format is 'key=value'. This argument can be specified multiple times. When the same key is also specified in the config file, the value from the command-line flag will overwrite the value from the config file.

options.name: py_module
options.help: Python modules to be available for import in the Ray workers. Each entry must be a path to a local directory.

options.name: cloud
options.help: The Anyscale Cloud of this workload. If not provided, the organization default will be used (or, if running in a workspace, the cloud of the workspace).

options.name: project
options.help: Named project to use for the service. If not provided, the default project for the cloud will be used (or, if running in a workspace, the project of the workspace).

options.name: versions
options.help: Defines the traffic and capacity percents per version. Capacity defaults to traffic.

options.name: tags
options.help: Tag in key=value (or key:value) format. Repeat to add multiple.

options.name: help
options.help: Show this message and exit.

sdk_commands.name: list
sdk_commands.docstring: List services or fetch a single service by ID.

parameters.name: service_id
parameters.docstring: If provided, returns just the service with this ID wrapped in a one-page iterator.

parameters.name: name
parameters.docstring: Substring to match against the service name.

parameters.name: state_filter
parameters.docstring: List of states to include. May be `ServiceState` enums or case-insensitive strings.

parameters.name: creator_id
parameters.docstring: Filter services by user ID.

parameters.name: cloud
parameters.docstring: Name of the Anyscale Cloud to search in.

parameters.name: project
parameters.docstring: Name of the Anyscale Project to search in.

parameters.name: include_archived
parameters.docstring: Include archived services (default: False).

parameters.name: tags_filter
parameters.docstring: Filter services by tags as a dict: \{key: [values...]\}. Values with the same key are ORed; keys are ANDed.

parameters.name: max_items
parameters.docstring: Maximum **total** number of items to yield (default: iterate all).

parameters.name: page_size
parameters.docstring: Number of items to fetch per API request (default: API default).

parameters.name: sort_field
parameters.docstring: Field to sort by (`NAME`, `STATUS`, `CREATED_AT`).

parameters.name: sort_order
parameters.docstring: Sort direction (`ASC` or `DESC`).

examples.python:
import anyscale
from anyscale.service.models import ServiceState

# Example: Get the first 50 running services
for svc in anyscale.service.list(max_items=50, state_filter=[ServiceState.RUNNING]):
    print(svc.name)

sdk_commands.name: deploy
sdk_commands.docstring:
Deploy a service.

    If no service with the provided name is running, one will be created, else the existing service will be updated.

    This command is asynchronous, so it always returns immediately.

    Returns the id of the deployed service.

parameters.name: configs
parameters.docstring: The config options defining the service.

parameters.name: in_place
parameters.docstring: Perform an in-place upgrade without starting a new cluster. This can be used for faster iteration during development but is *not* currently recommended for production deploys. This *cannot* be used to change cluster-level options such as image and compute config (they will be ignored).

parameters.name: canary_percent
parameters.docstring: The percentage of traffic to send to the canary version of the service (0-100). This can be used to manually shift traffic toward (or away from) the canary version. If not provided, traffic will be shifted incrementally toward the canary version until it reaches 100. Not supported when using --in-place. This is ignored when restarting a service or creating a new service.

parameters.name: max_surge_percent
parameters.docstring: Amount of excess capacity allowed to be used while updating the service (0-100). Defaults to 100. Not supported when using --in-place.

parameters.name: versions
parameters.docstring: Enable multi-version deployment by providing a JSON array of objects or a JSON object in text format. Defines the version name, traffic and capacity percents per version. Capacity defaults to traffic.

parameters.name: name
parameters.docstring: Name of the service. When running in a workspace, this defaults to the workspace name.

parameters.name: cloud
parameters.docstring: The Anyscale Cloud of this workload. If not provided, the organization default will be used (or, if running in a workspace, the cloud of the workspace).

parameters.name: project
parameters.docstring: Named project to use for the service. If not provided, the default project for the cloud will be used (or, if running in a workspace, the project of the workspace).

examples.python:
import anyscale
from anyscale.service.models import ServiceConfig

anyscale.service.deploy(
    ServiceConfig(
        name="my-service",
        applications=[
            {"import_path": "main:app"},
        ],
        working_dir=".",
    ),
    canary_percent=50,
)

models.name: ServiceConfig
models.docstring: Configuration options for a service.

fields.name: name
fields.docstring: Unique name of the service. When running inside a workspace, this defaults to the name of the workspace, else it is required.

fields.name: image_uri
fields.docstring: URI of an existing image. Exclusive with `containerfile`.

fields.name: containerfile
fields.docstring: The file path to a containerfile that will be built into an image before running the workload. Exclusive with `image_uri`.

fields.name: compute_config
fields.docstring: The name of an existing registered compute config or an inlined ComputeConfig or MultiResourceComputeConfig object.

fields.name: working_dir
fields.docstring: Directory that will be used as the working directory for the application. If a local directory is provided, it will be uploaded to cloud storage automatically. When running inside a workspace, this defaults to the current working directory ('.').

fields.name: excludes
fields.docstring: A list of file path globs that will be excluded when uploading local files for `working_dir`.

fields.name: requirements
fields.docstring: A list of pip requirements or a path to a `requirements.txt` file for the workload. Anyscale installs these dependencies on top of the image. If you run a workload from a workspace, the default is to use the workspace dependencies, but specifying this option overrides them.

fields.name: env_vars
fields.docstring: A dictionary of environment variables that will be set for the workload.

fields.name: py_modules
fields.docstring: A list of local directories or remote URIs that will be uploaded and added to the Python path.

fields.name: py_executable
fields.docstring: Specifies the executable used for running the Ray workers. It can include arguments as well.

fields.name: cloud
fields.docstring: The Anyscale Cloud to run this workload on. If not provided, the organization default will be used (or, if running in a workspace, the cloud of the workspace).

fields.name: project
fields.docstring: The project for the workload. If not provided, the default project for the cloud will be used (or, if running in a workspace, the project of the workspace).

fields.name: registry_login_secret
fields.docstring: A name or identifier of the secret containing credentials to authenticate to the docker registry hosting the image. This can only be used when 'image_uri' is specified and the image is not hosted on Anyscale.

fields.name: ray_version
fields.docstring: The Ray version (X.Y.Z) specified for this image specified by either an image URI or a containerfile. If you don't specify a Ray version, Anyscale defaults to the latest Ray version available at the time of the Anyscale CLI/SDK release.

fields.name: applications
fields.docstring: List of Ray Serve applications to run. At least one application must be specified. For details, see the Ray Serve config file format documentation: https://docs.ray.io/en/latest/serve/production-guide/config.html

fields.name: version_name
fields.docstring: Unique name of the version.

fields.name: query_auth_token_enabled
fields.docstring: Whether or not queries to this service is gated behind an authentication token. If `True`, an auth token is generated the first time the service is deployed. You can find the token in the UI or by fetching the status of the service.

fields.name: http_options
fields.docstring: HTTP options that will be passed to Ray Serve. See https://docs.ray.io/en/latest/serve/production-guide/config.html for supported options.

fields.name: grpc_options
fields.docstring: gRPC options that will be passed to Ray Serve. See https://docs.ray.io/en/latest/serve/production-guide/config.html for supported options.

fields.name: logging_config
fields.docstring: Logging options that will be passed to Ray Serve. See https://docs.ray.io/en/latest/serve/production-guide/config.html for supported options.

fields.name: ray_gcs_external_storage_config
fields.docstring: Configuration options for external storage for the Ray Global Control Store (GCS).

fields.name: tracing_config
fields.docstring: Configuration options for tracing.

fields.name: tags
fields.docstring: Tags to associate with the service.

examples.yaml:
name: my-service
applications:
  - import_path: main:app
image_uri: anyscale/image/my-image:1 # (Optional) Exclusive with `containerfile`.
containerfile: /path/to/Dockerfile # (Optional) Exclusive with `image_uri`.
compute_config: my-compute-config:1 # (Optional) An inline dictionary can also be provided.
working_dir: /path/to/working_dir # (Optional) If a local directory is provided, it will be uploaded automatically.
excludes: # (Optional) List of files to exclude from being packaged up for the service.
  - .git
  - .env
  - .DS_Store
  - __pycache__
requirements: # (Optional) List of requirements files to install. Can also be a path to a requirements.txt.
  - emoji==1.2.0
  - numpy==1.19.5
env_vars: # (Optional) Dictionary of environment variables to set in the service.
  MY_ENV_VAR: my_value
  ANOTHER_ENV_VAR: another_value
py_modules: # (Optional) A list of local directories or remote URIs that will be added to the Python path.
  - /path/to/my_module
  - s3://my_bucket/my_module
cloud: anyscale-prod # (Optional) The name of the Anyscale Cloud.
project: my-project # (Optional) The name of the Anyscale Project.
query_auth_token_enabled: True # (Optional) Whether or not to gate this service behind an auth token.
http_options:
  request_timeout_s: 60 # (Optional) Timeout for HTTP requests in seconds. Default is no timeout.
  keep_alive_timeout_s: 60 # (Optional) Timeout for HTTP keep-alive connections in seconds. Default is 5 seconds.
grpc_options:
  port: 50051 # (Optional) Port to listen on for gRPC requests. Default is 9000.
  grpc_servicer_functions: # (Optional) List of functions to expose as gRPC servicers.
    - my_module:my_function
logging_config: # (Optional) Configuration options for logging.
  encoding: JSON # JSON or TEXT.
  log_level: INFO
  enable_access_log: true
tracing_config: # (Optional) Configuration options for tracing.
  enabled: true
  exporter_import_path: my_module:custom_tracing_exporter
  sampling_ratio: 1.0
tags:
  team: serving
  cost-center: eng
examples.python:
from anyscale.service.models import ServiceConfig

config = ServiceConfig(
    name="my-service",
    working_dir=".",
    applications=[{"import_path": "main:app"}],
    # An inline `ComputeConfig` can also be provided.
    compute_config="my-compute-config:1",
    # A containerfile path can also be provided.
    image_uri="anyscale/image/my-image:1",
)

models.name: RayGCSExternalStorageConfig
models.docstring:
Configuration options for external storage for the Ray Global Control Store (GCS).

    When external storage is enabled for a service, it will be able to continue serving traffic if the
    head node goes down. Without external storage, the entire cluster will be restarted.

fields.name: enabled
fields.docstring: Enable or disable external storage. When `False`, the other fields are ignored.

fields.name: address
fields.docstring: Address to connect to the redis instance at. Defaults to the cloud-wide configuration.

fields.name: certificate_path
fields.docstring: Path to the TLS certificate file to use for authentication when using secure connections. Defaults to the cloud-wide configuration.

examples.yaml:
name: my-service
applications:
  - import_path: main:app
ray_gcs_external_storage_config:
  enabled: true
  address: http://redis-address:8888
  certificate_path: /etc/ssl/certs/ca-certificates.crt
examples.python:
from anyscale.service.models import ServiceConfig, RayGCSExternalStorageConfig

config = ServiceConfig(
    name="my-service",
    applications=[{"import_path": "main:app"}],
    ray_gcs_external_storage_config=RayGCSExternalStorageConfig(
        enabled=True,
        address="http://redis-address:8888",
        certificate_path="/etc/ssl/certs/ca-certificates.crt",
    ),
)

models.name: TracingConfig
models.docstring:
Configuration options for tracing.

    Tracing provides a way to collect telemetry on requests that are
    handled by your Serve application. If tracing is enabled, you can instrument
    your Serve application and export traces to a specific backend by configuring the
    `exporter_import_path` option. This feature is currently experimental.

fields.name: enabled
fields.docstring: Flag to enable tracing. When `True` and no `exporter_import_path` is defined, then the default tracing exporter will be used to export traces to serve logs. When `False`, the other fields are ignored.

fields.name: exporter_import_path
fields.docstring: Path to exporter function. Should be of the form "module.submodule_1...submodule_n.export_tracing". This is equivalent to "from module.submodule_1...submodule_n import export_tracing. Exporter function takes no arguments and returns List[SpanProcessor].

fields.name: sampling_ratio
fields.docstring: Ratio of traces to record and export. Reducing the ratio can help with minimizing serving latency and storage overhead.

examples.yaml:
name: my-service
applications:
  - import_path: main:app
tracing_config:
  enabled: true
  exporter_import_path: "my_module:custom_tracing_exporter"
  sampling_ratio: 1.0
examples.python:
from anyscale.service.models import ServiceConfig, TracingConfig

config = ServiceConfig(
    name="my-service",
    applications=[{"import_path": "main:app"}],
    tracing_config=TracingConfig(
        enabled=True,
        exporter_import_path="my_module:custom_tracing_exporter",
        sampling_ratio=1.0,
    ),
)

models.name: ServiceStatus
models.docstring: Current status of a service.

fields.name: name
fields.docstring: Unique name of the service.

fields.name: id
fields.docstring: Unique ID of the service (generated when the service is first deployed).

fields.name: state
fields.docstring: Current state of the service.

fields.name: query_url
fields.docstring: URL used to query the service.

fields.name: creator
fields.docstring: Email of the user or entity that created the service.

fields.name: query_auth_token
fields.docstring: Query auth token required to query the service (if any). Must be passed in as a header like: `Authorization: Bearer <token>`.

fields.name: primary_version
fields.docstring: Primary version of the service. During a rollout, this is the service version that was running prior to the deploy.

fields.name: canary_version
fields.docstring: Canary version of the service (the one that is being deployed). When there is no rollout ongoing, this will be `None`.

fields.name: project
fields.docstring: Name of the project that this service version belongs to.

fields.name: versions
fields.docstring: All active versions of the service. For multi-version services, this contains all versions with their traffic weights.

examples.python:
import anyscale
from anyscale.service.models import ServiceStatus

status: ServiceStatus = anyscale.service.status("my-service")
examples.cli:
$ anyscale service status -n my-service
name: my-service
id: service2_uz6l8yhy2as5wrer3shzj6kh67
state: RUNNING
primary_version:
  id: 601bd56c4b
  state: RUNNING
  weight: 100

models.name: ServiceState
models.docstring: Current state of a service.

members.name: RUNNING
members.docstring: The service is running and healthy.

members.name: UPDATING
members.docstring: One of the service versions or the networking stack is updating.

members.name: ROLLING_OUT
members.docstring: The service is rolling out to a new canary version.

members.name: ROLLING_BACK
members.docstring: The service is rolling back to the primary version.

members.name: UNHEALTHY
members.docstring: The service has become unhealthy due to an unexpected issue such as a node failure. In most cases these issues should automatically recover.

members.name: UNKNOWN
members.docstring: The CLI/SDK received an unexpected state from the API server. In most cases, this means you need to update the CLI.

members.name: SYSTEM_FAILURE
members.docstring: An unexpected error occurred in the Anyscale stack. Please reach out to customer support immediately.

models.name: ServiceVersionStatus

fields.name: state
fields.docstring: Current state of the service version.

fields.name: weight
fields.docstring: Current canary weight of the service version. This is `100` if there is no ongoing rollout.

fields.name: created_at
fields.docstring: Creation time of the service version.

fields.name: config
fields.docstring: Configuration of this service version.

examples.python:

models.name: ServiceVersionState

cli_commands.name: create
cli_commands.help:
Create a new version of a compute config from a YAML file.

(1) To use the **new schema** defined at https://docs.anyscale.com/reference/compute-config-api#computeconfig, use the -f/--config-file flag:

`anyscale compute-config create -f new_schema_config.yaml`

(2) To use the **old schema** defined at https://docs.anyscale.com/reference/compute-config-api/#createclustercomputeconfig-legacy, use the positional argument:

`anyscale compute-config create old_schema_config.yaml`

options.name: name
options.help: Name for the created compute config. This should *not* include a version tag. If a name is not provided, an anonymous compute config is generated. Anonymous compute configs are not accessible in the UI and can only be referenced by their ID.

options.name: config_file
options.help: Path to a YAML config file defining the compute config. Schema: https://docs.anyscale.com/reference/compute-config-api#computeconfig.

options.name: help
options.help: Show this message and exit.

examples.cli:
$ anyscale compute-config create -n my-compute-config -f compute_config.yaml
(anyscale +3.7s) Created compute config: 'my-compute-config:1'
(anyscale +3.7s) View the compute config in the UI: 'https://console.anyscale.com/v2/...'

$cat compute_config.yaml
head_node:
  instance_type: m5.8xlarge
worker_nodes:
- instance_type: m5.8xlarge
  min_nodes: 5
  max_nodes: 5
  market_type: ON_DEMAND # (Optional) Defaults to ON_DEMAND
- instance_type: g4dn.xlarge
  min_nodes: 1
  max_nodes: 10
  market_type: PREFER_SPOT # (Optional) Defaults to ON_DEMAND
min_resources: # (Optional) Defaults to no minimum.
  CPU: 1
  GPU: 1
max_resources: # (Optional) Defaults to no maximum.
  CPU: 6
  GPU: 1

cli_commands.name: get
cli_commands.help:
Get the details of a compute config.

The name can contain an optional version, e.g., 'name:version'. If no version is provided, the latest one will be returned.

options.name: name
options.help: Name of the compute config.

options.name: include_archived
options.help: Include archived compute configurations.

options.name: old_format
options.help: Output the config in the old format: https://docs.anyscale.com/reference/python-sdk/models#createclustercomputeconfig.

options.name: help
options.help: Show this message and exit.

examples.cli:
$ anyscale compute-config get -n my-compute-config
name: my-compute-config:1
id: cpt_buthu4glxj3azv4e287jad3ya3
config:
  cloud: aviary-prod-us-east-1
  head_node:
    instance_type: m5.8xlarge
    resources:
      CPU: 0
      GPU: 0
  worker_nodes:
  - instance_type: m5.8xlarge
    name: m5.8xlarge
    min_nodes: 5
    max_nodes: 5
    market_type: ON_DEMAND
  - instance_type: g4dn.xlarge
    name: g4dn.xlarge
    min_nodes: 1
    max_nodes: 10
    market_type: PREFER_SPOT
  min_resources:
    CPU: 1
    GPU: 1
  max_resources:
    CPU: 6
    GPU: 1
  enable_cross_zone_scaling: false
  flags: {}

cli_commands.name: archive
cli_commands.help: Archive all versions of a specified compute config.

options.name: name
options.help: Name of the compute config to archive.

options.name: compute_config_id
options.help: ID of the compute config to archive. Alternative to name.

options.name: help
options.help: Show this message and exit.

examples.cli:
$ anyscale compute-config archive -n my-compute-config
(anyscale +2.3s) Compute config is successfully archived.

sdk_commands.name: create
sdk_commands.docstring:
Create a new version of a compute config.

    Returns the full name of the registered compute config, including the version.

parameters.name: config
parameters.docstring: The config options defining the compute config.

parameters.name: name
parameters.docstring: The name of the compute config. This should *not* include a version tag. If a name is not provided, one will be automatically generated.

examples.python:
import anyscale
from anyscale.compute_config.models import ComputeConfig, HeadNodeConfig, MarketType, WorkerNodeGroupConfig

single_deployment_compute_config = ComputeConfig(
    head_node=HeadNodeConfig(
        instance_type="m5.8xlarge",
    ),
    worker_nodes=[
        WorkerNodeGroupConfig(
            instance_type="m5.8xlarge",
            min_nodes=5,
            max_nodes=5,
        ),
        WorkerNodeGroupConfig(
            instance_type="m5.4xlarge",
            min_nodes=1,
            max_nodes=10,
            market_type=MarketType.SPOT,
        ),
    ],
)
full_name: str = anyscale.compute_config.create(single_deployment_compute_config, name="my-single-deployment-compute-config")

multi_deployment_compute_config = MultiResourceComputeConfig(
    configs=[
        ComputeConfig(
            cloud_resource="vm-aws-us-west-1",
            head_node=HeadNodeConfig(
                instance_type="m5.2xlarge",
            ),
            worker_nodes=[
                WorkerNodeGroupConfig(
                    instance_type="m5.4xlarge",
                    min_nodes=1,
                    max_nodes=10,
                ),
            ],
        ),
        ComputeConfig(
            cloud_resource="vm-aws-us-west-2",
            head_node=HeadNodeConfig(
                instance_type="m5.2xlarge",
            ),
            worker_nodes=[
                WorkerNodeGroupConfig(
                    instance_type="m5.4xlarge",
                    min_nodes=1,
                    max_nodes=10,
                ),
            ],

sdk_commands.name: archive
sdk_commands.docstring:
Archive a compute config and all of its versions.

    The name can contain an optional version, e.g., 'name:version'.
    If no version is provided, the latest one will be archived.

    Once a compute config is archived, its name will no longer be usable in the organization.

parameters.docstring: Name of the compute config.

examples.python:
import anyscale

anyscale.compute_config.archive(name="my-compute-config")

models.name: ComputeConfig
models.docstring: Compute configuration for instance types and cloud resources for a cluster with a single cloud resource.

fields.name: cloud
fields.docstring: The Anyscale Cloud to run this workload on. If not provided, the organization default will be used (or, if running in a workspace, the cloud of the workspace).

fields.name: cloud_resource
fields.docstring: The cloud resource to use for this workload. Defaults to the primary cloud resource of the Cloud.

fields.name: head_node
fields.docstring: Configuration options for the head node of the cluster. Defaults to the cloud's default head node configuration.

fields.name: worker_nodes
fields.docstring: Configuration options for the worker nodes of the cluster. If not provided, worker nodes will be automatically selected based on logical resource requests. To use a head-node only cluster, pass `[]` here.

fields.name: min_resources
fields.docstring: Total minimum logical resources across all nodes in the cluster. Resources omitted from this field have no minimum.

fields.name: max_resources
fields.docstring: Total maximum logical resources across all nodes in the cluster. Resources omitted from this field have no maximum.

fields.name: zones
fields.docstring: Availability zones to consider for this cluster. Defaults to all zones in the cloud's region. By default all instances with user workloads scheduled on them will run in the same zone to save cost, unless `enable_cross_zone_scaling` is set.

fields.name: enable_cross_zone_scaling
fields.docstring: Allow instances in the cluster to be run across multiple zones. This is recommended when running production services (for fault-tolerance in a zone failure scenario). It is not recommended for workloads that have a large amount of inter-zone communication due to the possibility of higher costs and degraded performance. When false, all instances with user workloads scheduled on them (e.g. all worker nodes in multi-node clusters) will run in the same zone to save cost.

fields.name: advanced_instance_config
fields.docstring: Advanced instance configurations that will be passed through to the cloud provider.

fields.name: flags
fields.docstring: Cluster-level flags specifying advanced or experimental options.

fields.name: auto_select_worker_config
fields.docstring: Allow worker groups to be automatically configured based on the workload's logical resource requests. When false, worker groups must be explicitly configured.

examples.yaml:
cloud: my-cloud
zones: # (Optional) Defaults to to all zones in a region.
  - us-west-2a
  - us-west-2b
head_node:
  instance_type: m5.8xlarge
worker_nodes:
- instance_type: m5.8xlarge
  min_nodes: 5
  max_nodes: 5
  market_type: PREFER_SPOT # (Optional) Defaults to ON_DEMAND
- instance_type: g5.4xlarge
  min_nodes: 1
  max_nodes: 10
  market_type: SPOT # (Optional) Defaults to ON_DEMAND
min_resources: # (Optional) Defaults to no minimum.
  CPU: 1
  GPU: 1
  CUSTOM_RESOURCE: 0
max_resources: # (Optional) Defaults to no maximum.
  CPU: 6
  GPU: 10
  CUSTOM_RESOURCE: 10
enable_cross_zone_scaling: true # (Optional) Defaults to false.
advanced_instance_config: # (Optional) Defaults to no advanced configurations.
  # AWS specific configuration example
  BlockDeviceMappings:
    - DeviceName: DEVICE_NAME
      Ebs:
        VolumeSize: VOLUME_SIZE
        DeleteOnTermination: DELETE_ON_TERMINATION
  IamInstanceProfile:
    Arn: IAM_INSTANCE_PROFILE_ARN
  NetworkInterfaces:
    - SubnetId: SUBNET_ID
      Groups:
        - SECURITY_GROUP_ID
      AssociatePublicIpAddress: ASSOCIATE_PUBLIC_IP
  TagSpecifications:
    - ResourceType: RESOURCE_TYPE
      Tags:
        - Key: TAG_KEY
          Value: TAG_VALUE
  # GCP specific configuration example
  instance_properties:
    disks:
      - boot: BOOT_OPTION
        auto_delete: AUTO_DELETE_OPTION
        initialize_params:
          disk_size_gb: DISK_SIZE_GB
    service_accounts:
      - email: SERVICE_ACCOUNT_EMAIL
        scopes:
          - SCOPE_URL
    network_interfaces:
      - subnetwork: SUBNETWORK_URL
        access_configs:
          - type: ACCESS_CONFIG_TYPE
    labels:
      LABEL_KEY: LABEL_VALUE

models.name: MultiResourceComputeConfig
models.docstring: EXPERIMENTAL. Compute configuration for a cluster with multiple possible cloud resources.

fields.name: cloud
fields.docstring: The Anyscale Cloud to run this workload on. If not provided, the organization default will be used (or, if running in a workspace, the cloud of the workspace).

fields.name: configs
fields.docstring: List of compute configurations, one for each cloud resource.

fields.name: flags
fields.docstring: Flags specifying advanced or experimental options that should be applied to all cloud resources. Flags specified in the individual compute configurations will override these flags.

examples.yaml:
cloud: my-cloud
configs:
- cloud_resource: vm-aws-us-west-1
  head_node:
    instance_type: m5.2xlarge
  worker_nodes:
  - instance_type: m5.4xlarge
    min_nodes: 1
    max_nodes: 10
- cloud_resource: vm-aws-us-west-2
  head_node:
    instance_type: m5.2xlarge
  worker_nodes:
  - instance_type: m5.4xlarge
    min_nodes: 1
    max_nodes: 10

models.name: HeadNodeConfig
models.docstring: Configuration options for the head node of a cluster.

fields.name: instance_type
fields.docstring: Cloud provider instance type, e.g., `m5.2xlarge` on AWS or `n2-standard-8` on GCP. Defaults to 'custom' when physical_resources is provided.

fields.name: resources
fields.docstring: Logical resources that will be available on this node. Defaults to match the physical resources of the instance type.

fields.name: physical_resources
fields.docstring: Physical resources for custom instance types (free pod shapes). Explicitly defines CPU, memory, and GPU resources.

fields.name: labels
fields.docstring: Labels to associate the node with for scheduling purposes. Defaults to the list of Ray & Anyscale default labels.

fields.name: advanced_instance_config
fields.docstring: Advanced instance configurations that will be passed through to the cloud provider.

fields.name: flags
fields.docstring: Node-level flags specifying advanced or experimental options.

fields.name: cloud_deployment
fields.docstring: Cloud deployment selectors for a node group; one or more selectors may be passed to target a specific deployment from all of a cloud's deployments.

examples.yaml:
head_node:
  instance_type: m5.8xlarge

models.name: WorkerNodeGroupConfig
models.docstring:
Configuration options for a worker node group in a cluster.

    Clusters can have multiple worker node groups that use different instance types or configurations.

fields.name: instance_type
fields.docstring: Cloud provider instance type, e.g., `m5.2xlarge` on AWS or `n2-standard-8` on GCP. Defaults to 'custom' when physical_resources is provided.

fields.name: resources
fields.docstring: Logical resources that will be available on this node. Defaults to match the physical resources of the instance type.

fields.name: physical_resources
fields.docstring: Physical resources for custom instance types (free pod shapes). Explicitly defines CPU, memory, and GPU resources.

fields.name: labels
fields.docstring: Labels to associate the node with for scheduling purposes. Defaults to the list of Ray & Anyscale default labels.

fields.name: advanced_instance_config
fields.docstring: Advanced instance configurations that will be passed through to the cloud provider.

fields.name: flags
fields.docstring: Node-level flags specifying advanced or experimental options.

fields.name: cloud_deployment
fields.docstring: Cloud deployment selectors for a node group; one or more selectors may be passed to target a specific deployment from all of a cloud's deployments.

fields.name: name
fields.docstring: Unique name of this worker group. Defaults to a human-friendly representation of the instance type.

fields.name: min_nodes
fields.docstring: Minimum number of nodes of this type that will be kept running in the cluster.

fields.name: max_nodes
fields.docstring: Maximum number of nodes of this type that can be running in the cluster.

fields.name: market_type
fields.docstring: The type of instances to use (see `MarketType` enum values for details).

examples.yaml:
worker_nodes:
- instance_type: m5.8xlarge
  min_nodes: 5
  max_nodes: 5
- instance_type: m5.4xlarge
  min_nodes: 1
  max_nodes: 10
  market_type: SPOT
examples.python:
from anyscale.compute_config.models import ComputeConfig, MarketType, WorkerNodeGroupConfig

config = ComputeConfig(
    worker_nodes=[
        WorkerNodeGroupConfig(
            instance_type="m5.8xlarge",
            min_nodes=5,
            max_nodes=5,
        ),
        WorkerNodeGroupConfig(
            instance_type="m5.4xlarge",
            min_nodes=1,
            max_nodes=10,
            market_type=MarketType.SPOT,
        ),
    ],
)

models.name: MarketType
models.docstring: Market type of instances to use (on-demand vs. spot).

members.name: PREFER_SPOT
members.docstring: Prefer to use spot instances, but fall back to on-demand if necessary. If on-demand instances are running and spot instances become available, the on-demand instances will be evicted and replaced with spot instances.

models.name: CloudDeployment

fields.name: provider

models.name: PhysicalResources

options.help: An endpoint for cloud storage, e.g. used to override the default cloud storage scheme's endpoint (e.g. for S3, this would be passed to the AWS_ENDPOINT_URL environment variable).

options.help: The region of the cloud storage bucket. If not provided, the region of the cloud will be used to access the cloud storage bucket.

options.name: nfs_mount_target
options.help: A comma-separated value representing a (zone, mount target) tuple, e.g. us-west-2a,1.2.3.4 (may be provided multiple times, one for each zone). If only one value is provided (e.g. 1.2.3.4), then that value will be used for all zones.

options.name: nfs_mount_path
options.help: The path of the NFS server to mount from (e.g. nfs-target-address/nfs-path will be mounted).

options.name: persistent_volume_claim
options.help: For Kubernetes deployments only, the name of the persistent volume claim used to mount shared storage into pods. Mutually exclusive with NFS configurations.

options.name: csi_ephemeral_volume_driver
options.help: For Kubernetes deployments only, the CSI ephemeral volume driver used to mount shared storage into pods. Mutually exclusive with NFS configurations.

options.name: kubernetes_zones

options.name: anyscale_operator_iam_identity
options.help: On the Kubernetes compute stack, the cloud provider IAM identity federated with the Anyscale Operator's kubernetes service account, which will be used by Anyscale control plane for validation during Anyscale Operator bootstrap in the dataplane. IN AWS EKS, this is the ARN of the IAM role. For GCP GKE, this is the service account email.

options.name: enable_log_ingestion
options.help: If --enable-log-ingestion is specified for a cloud, it will enable the log viewing and querying UI features for the clusters on this cloud. This will enable easier debugging. The logs produced by the clusters will be sent from the data plane to the control plane. Anyscale does not share this data with any third party or use it for any purpose other than serving the log UI for the customer. The log will be stored at most 30 days.Please note by disable this feature again, Anyscale doesn't delete the logs that have already been ingested. Your clusters may incur extra data transfer cost from the cloud provider by enabling this feature.

options.name: enable_system_cluster
options.help: Enable or disable system cluster functionality.

options.name: spec_file
options.help: Provide a path to a specification file.

options.name: help
options.help: Show this message and exit.

examples.cli:

--enable-log-ingestion is specified. [...] If you are sure you want to enable this feature, please type "consent": consent
Output
(anyscale +7.3s) Successfully updated log ingestion configuration for cloud, cloud_id to True
--enable-system-cluster is specified. [...] Are you sure you want to enable system cluster? [y/N]: y
Output
(anyscale +11.4s) Successfully enabled system cluster for cloud cloud_id

$ anyscale cloud config update --cloud-id cloud_id --spec-file iam.yaml
Output
(anyscale +2.1s) Successfully updated cloud configuration for cloud my-cloud (resource: cldrsrc_xyz123)

$ anyscale cloud config update --cloud-id cloud_id --resource shared-usw2 --spec-file iam.yaml
Output
(anyscale +2.1s) Successfully updated cloud configuration for cloud my-cloud (resource: cldrsrc_abc456)

$ anyscale cloud config update --cloud-id cloud_id --cloud-resource-id cldrsrc_xyz123 --spec-file iam.yaml

(anyscale +2.1s) Successfully updated cloud configuration for cloud my-cloud (resource: cldrsrc_xyz123)

sdk_commands.name: add_collaborators
sdk_commands.docstring:
Batch add collaborators to a cloud.

    :param cloud: The cloud to add users to.
    :param collaborators: The list of collaborators to add to the cloud.

parameters.name: cloud
parameters.docstring: The cloud to add users to.

parameters.name: collaborators
parameters.docstring: The list of collaborators to add to the cloud.

examples.python:
import anyscale
from anyscale.cloud.models import CloudPermissionLevel, CreateCloudCollaborator

anyscale.cloud.add_collaborators(
    cloud="cloud_name",
    collaborators=[
        CreateCloudCollaborator(
            email="test1@anyscale.com",
            permission_level=CloudPermissionLevel.WRITE,
        ),
        CreateCloudCollaborator(
            email="test2@anyscale.com",
            permission_level=CloudPermissionLevel.READONLY,
        ),
    ],
)

parameters.docstring: The name of the cloud to retrieve.

import anyscale

# Get a cloud by name
cloud_by_name = anyscale.cloud.get(name="cloud_name")

sdk_commands.name: terminate_system_cluster
sdk_commands.docstring:
Terminate the system cluster for the specified cloud.

    :param cloud: The name of the cloud whose system cluster should be terminated.
    :param wait: If True, wait for the system cluster to be terminated before returning. Defaults to False.
    :return: ID of the terminated system cluster.

parameters.name: cloud_id
parameters.docstring: The ID of the cloud whose system cluster should be terminated.

parameters.name: wait
parameters.docstring: If True, wait for the system cluster to be terminated before returning. Defaults to False.

examples.python:
import anyscale

# Terminate the system cluster for the cloud with the specified ID
anyscale.cloud.terminate_system_cluster(cloud_id="cloud_id", wait=True)

models.name: Cloud
models.docstring: Minimal Cloud resource model.

fields.name: name
fields.docstring: Name of this Cloud.

fields.name: id
fields.docstring: Unique identifier for this Cloud.

fields.name: provider
fields.docstring: Cloud provider (AWS, GCP, AZURE, GENERIC) or UNKNOWN if not recognized.

fields.name: compute_stack
fields.docstring: The compute stack associated with this cloud's primary cloud resource, or UNKNOWN if not recognized.

fields.name: region
fields.docstring: Region for this Cloud.

fields.name: created_at
fields.docstring: When the Cloud was created.

fields.name: is_default
fields.docstring: Whether this is the default cloud.

fields.name: is_aggregated_logs_enabled
fields.docstring: Whether aggregated logs are enabled for this cloud.

examples.python:
from datetime import datetime
from anyscale.cloud.models import Cloud, CloudProvider, ComputeStack

cloud = Cloud(
    name="my-cloud",
    id="cloud-123",
    provider="AWS",  # This will be validated as CloudProvider.AWS
    region="us-west-2",
    created_at=datetime.now(),
    is_default=True,
    compute_stack="VM"  # This will be validated as ComputeStack.VM
)

fields.docstring: Permission level the added user should have for the cloud(one of: WRITE,READONLY

examples.python:
import anyscale
from anyscale.cloud.models import CloudPermissionLevel, CreateCloudCollaborator

create_cloud_collaborator = CreateCloudCollaborator(
   # Email of the user to be added as a collaborator
    email="test@anyscale.com",
    # Permission level for the user to the cloud (CloudPermissionLevel.WRITE, CloudPermissionLevel.READONLY)
    permission_level=CloudPermissionLevel.READONLY,
)
